title: Multiple volume_path options under a single user context
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

There needs to be a way to specify multiple volume_path options under a single user context.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Attach multiple classes of storage (single HDD, RAID10 HDD array, NVME SSD mirror) to Podman host

**Describe the results you received:**

Can point volume_path only towards a single path option at a time.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Need to have a way to have multiple volume_paths in a single user context and the ability to specify which volumes go to what volume_path.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

A very ugly workaround is to have multiple users running podman with each user having volume_path set to one of the many available storage options. This is less than workable as it's not at all black and white that ALL containers of any particular stack would be best fit a single specific storage option. Often you have a single stack of containers where some volumes really do benefit from fast storage while many others do not.

And obviously this significantly increases administrational overhead.

**Output of `podman version`:**

API Version: 4.1.1

comments.body: Can you just use regular bind mounts instead, `-v /path/on/host:/path/in/container`?

> Can you just use regular bind mounts instead, `-v /path/on/host:/path/in/container`?

Yes, bind mounts work, but bind mounts are not volumes.
Depends on what you are doing, `podman volume create`  without specific mount options will result in a normal bind mount under the volume path.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I really do not know what you are after here @dnv 
Could you give us a podman example of what you want?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we never heard back, closing, Reopen if you have more feedback.
